file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 9.5/10 (0 FAIL, 1 WARN, 11 PASS)

rules.detail: No `.unwrap()` calls present anywhere in this file.

rules.rule_name: No unsafe blocks without justification comment
rules.detail: No `unsafe` blocks present in this file.
No functions are defined in this lib root, so no error-handling surface exists here.

rules.rule_name: Derive common traits on public types
rules.detail: No types are declared in this file; trait derivations are not applicable.
No functions or types with lifetime parameters are present in this file.
No clippy-relevant patterns (manual loops, unnecessary clones, etc.) exist in this declaration-only file.
No `panic!` macros present. This is a library crate root with only module declarations.

Module hierarchy is clean and logical: six domain-specific public modules (cookie, handshake, messages, replay, session, timers) are properly declared with `pub mod`, and `rustguard_crypto` is cleanly re-exported as `crypto` via `pub use`. The `no_std` conditional attribute and explicit `extern crate alloc` are idiomatic for a portable library crate.

None of the six public module declarations or the `pub use rustguard_crypto as crypto` re-export have `///` documentation comments. As the public API entry point for the crate, each public item should carry at least a one-line summary to aid users of the library.
No secrets, keys, tokens, or credentials present in this file.
No `mem::forget`, raw pointer manipulation, or `Drop` implementations in this file. The `no_std` + `alloc` pattern is correctly applied for heap usage without the standard library.
No concurrency primitives, shared state, or thread-related constructs are introduced in this file. `Send`/`Sync` bounds are not applicable at this layer.

suggestions.description: Add `///` doc comments to each public module declaration and the crypto re-export so that `cargo doc` generates meaningful API documentation for library consumers.
